facts.text: Dolphins have two stomachs — one for food and one for digestion! They also use tools like sponges to protect their snouts!
Dolphins can recognize themselves in mirrors, which shows they have self-awareness similar to humans and great apes!
A dolphin can hold its breath for up to 10 minutes! They breathe through a blowhole on top of their head!
Dolphins sleep with one eye open! Half of their brain stays awake to watch for predators and keep breathing!
Dogs have 10,000 times better sense of smell than humans! Some dogs can detect diseases like cancer!
A dog's nose print is as unique as a human fingerprint! No two dogs have the same nose pattern!
Dogs can understand up to 250 words and gestures! The average dog is as smart as a two-year-old child!
When dogs wag their tail to the right, it usually means they're happy! To the left can indicate fear!
Cats spend 70% of their lives sleeping! That's about 13 to 16 hours a day!
A cat's purr vibrates at a frequency that helps heal bones and reduce pain!
Cats have over 20 different vocalizations! They can make over 100 different sounds!
A cat can jump up to six times its body length in a single leap! That's like a human jumping 30 feet!
Elephants are the only animals that can't jump! But they're excellent swimmers despite their massive size!
An elephant's trunk has over 40,000 muscles! It's more dexterous than human fingers!
Elephants can recognize themselves in mirrors, showing high intelligence and self-awareness!
Baby elephants suck their trunks for comfort, similar to how human babies suck their thumbs!
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood! Two hearts pump blood to the gills, one to the body!
An octopus can change its color and texture in less than a second to blend with its surroundings!
Octopuses have nine brains! One central brain and a mini brain in each of their eight arms!
They can squeeze through any gap larger than their beak, which is the only hard part of their body!
Sloths can hold their breath for up to 40 minutes! They slow their heart rate to survive underwater!
A sloth's stomach takes up a third of its body weight and can take 30 days to digest a single leaf!
Sloths descend from their trees only once a week to poop! They lose a third of their body weight in one trip!
Despite being slow on land, sloths are surprisingly strong swimmers! They can hold their breath for 40 minutes!
Owls can rotate their heads 270 degrees! They can't move their eyes because they're shaped like tubes!
A owl's eyes are fixed in place, so they have to turn their entire head to look around!
Some owl species can fly in complete silence! Their wing feathers have special edges that muffle sound!
Owls swallow their prey whole and later cough up pellets containing the bones and fur they couldn't digest!
Crows can recognize and remember human faces for years! They can tell if someone is a friend or foe!
A crow's brain is relatively the same size as a chimpanzee brain! They're among the smartest birds!
Crows use tools to get food! They can bend wire into hooks and drop pebbles to raise water levels!
Some crows hold funerals! They gather around dead crows to learn about dangers in their area!
A baby kangaroo is called a joey and it's only the size of a jellybean when born!
Kangaroos can't walk backwards! They only move forward, which is why they're on Australia's coat of arms!
When kangaroos hop, they use less energy than a cat or dog running at the same speed!
A group of kangaroos is called a mob! The mob is led by the largest male, called a boomer!
Some butterflies can travel 3,000 miles during migration, crossing the entire Atlantic Ocean!
Butterflies taste with their feet! Their taste sensors are located on their leg cells!
A butterfly's lifecycle is called metamorphosis! It has four stages: egg, caterpillar, chrysalis, adult!
Butterflies have been on Earth for over 50 million years, since the time of the dinosaurs!
Flamingos can run up to 35 miles per hour on the ground and prefer living in large groups of thousands!
Flamingos are pink because of the beta-carotene in the shrimp and algae they eat!
A flamingo can only eat with its head upside down! Their special beaks are designed for this!
Flamingos are one of the few birds that can actually swim! Many other birds avoid deep water!
Koalas have fingerprints almost identical to human fingerprints! Even experts can mistake them!
Koalas sleep up to 22 hours a day! Their diet of eucalyptus leaves gives them very little energy!
Baby koalas eat their mother's poop! This helps them develop the bacteria needed to digest eucalyptus!
Koalas are marsupials, meaning they carry their babies in pouches! The pouch opens toward the mother's back!
Ants can carry objects 50 times their own body weight! They're one of the strongest creatures on Earth!
Ants don't have lungs! They breathe through tiny holes all over their bodies called spiracles!
Ants can survive underwater! They can close their spiracles and hold their breath for a long time!
There are over 12,000 known species of ants, and they make up about 15% of all animal biomass on land!
Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! Their unique ball-and-socket shoulder joints allow this!
A hummingbird's heart beats over 1,200 times per minute during flight! At rest it's still 250 beats per minute!
Hummingbirds eat twice their body weight in nectar every day! They visit hundreds of flowers daily!
A hummingbird's brain is 4.2% of its body weight, the largest proportion of any bird! They can remember every flower they've visited!
Starfish don't have a brain! They process everything through their nervous system spread across their arms!
If a starfish loses an arm, it can grow it back! Sometimes the arm grows into a whole new starfish!
Starfish don't have blood! They use filtered sea water to pump nutrients through their bodies!
Starfish have eyes at the end of each arm! They can't see details but they can sense light and dark!
Emperor penguins can dive deeper than any other bird, reaching depths of over 1,800 feet!
A emperor penguin egg sits on the father's feet for two months! He doesn't eat and endures temperatures of -50°C!
Penguins can drink salt water because they have a special gland that filters salt from their blood!
The Galapagos penguin is the only penguin species found north of the equator! They live in tropical waters!
A tiger's night vision is 6 times better than a human's! They hunt mostly during dawn and dusk!
No two tigers have the same stripe pattern! Like human fingerprints, each tiger is completely unique!
A tiger's roar can be heard from 2 miles away! It communicates territorial boundaries to other tigers!
Tigers are excellent swimmers! Unlike most cats, they love the water and can swim for miles!
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping so they don't drift apart! They're incredibly social creatures!
Otters have the densest fur of any mammal, with up to a million hairs per square inch!
A group of otters in water is called a raft! The largest raft can have over 1,000 otters!
Otters can close their ears and nose underwater! They can stay submerged for nearly 5 minutes!
A fox's pupils are vertical, similar to cats! This helps them hunt small prey more effectively!
Foxes can hear a mouse moving under 3 feet of snow! They use a special pouncing technique to catch it!
Red foxes can adapt to almost any habitat! They live on every continent except Antarctica!
A fox's tail is called a brush! It helps them balance and also keeps them warm during cold nights!
Elephant seals can hold their breath for 2 hours! They dive deeper than almost any other marine mammal!
Seals have a layer of blubber under their skin that can be up to 6 inches thick!
A seal's whiskers are incredibly sensitive! They can detect vibrations in the water from fish swimming nearby!
Leopard seals can reach speeds of 25 mph underwater! They're one of the fastest seals in the ocean!
Llamas are very clean animals! They tend to use the same spot as a toilet, away from where they eat!
A llama can carry up to 30% of its body weight for up to 15 miles! They're perfect pack animals!
Llamas are social and get lonely if kept alone! They often bond closely with other llamas!
Llamas communicate by humming! Each hum can mean something different, from hunger to contentment!
Frogs don't drink water! They absorb it through their skin! Their belly is specially designed to soak up moisture!
Some frogs can freeze completely solid during winter and thaw out alive in spring! Their blood has special antifreeze proteins!
The glass frog has transparent skin on its belly, so you can see its heart, liver, and intestines!
Frogs shed their skin regularly and often eat the shed skin! Nothing goes to waste!
When rabbits are really happy, they do a twist in the air called a binky! It's an expression of pure joy!
A rabbit's teeth never stop growing! They need to chew on hay and vegetables to keep them worn down!
Rabbits can see nearly 360 degrees! Their only blind spot is directly in front of their nose!
Baby rabbits are called kits! A mother rabbit can have up to 12 babies in one litter!
Parrots have curved beaks specially adapted for cracking nuts and seeds! They can exert incredible force!
Some parrots can live over 80 years! The oldest known parrot was a macaw named Cookie, who lived to 82!
Parrots are one of the few animals that can dance to music! They sync their movements to rhythm naturally!
A parrot's tongue is thick and fleshy! It helps them mimic human speech by shaping sound in their throat!
A giraffe's tongue is 20 inches long and completely black! This protects it from sunburn while eating!
Giraffes only need 30 minutes of sleep per day! They often sleep standing up in short bursts!
Each giraffe has a unique pattern of spots, just like human fingerprints! No two giraffes are alike!
A giraffe's kick is so powerful it can kill a lion! They use their long necks as weapons too!
Sharks have been on Earth for over 400 million years! They existed before trees and dinosaurs!
Great white sharks can detect a single drop of blood in 25 gallons of water! That's incredible sensitivity!
Most sharks never stop swimming! If they stop, they sink because they don't have swim bladders!
Sharks can go through up to 30,000 teeth in their lifetime! They replace teeth every few weeks!
Horses can sleep both lying down and standing up! They have a locking system in their legs!
A horse's memory is extremely long! They can remember places and other horses for many years!
Horses have 10 different muscles in their ears! They can rotate their ears 180 degrees independently!
Newborn foals can run within a few hours of being born! This is essential for escaping predators!
Despite being called carnivores, 99% of a panda's diet is bamboo! They can't properly digest meat!
Giant pandas spend 12 hours every day eating bamboo! They consume up to 38 kg daily!
A newborn panda is about the size of a stick of butter! Adults weigh up to 250 pounds!
Pandas have a special wrist bone that acts like a thumb! It helps them grip bamboo stalks!
Chickens dream when they sleep! They experience REM sleep just like humans and other mammals!
A henmother calls to her chicks while they're still inside the egg! The chicks chirp back!
Chickens have over 30 distinct vocalizations! They have unique alarm calls for different predators!
A chicken can recognize over 100 different faces! They remember people and other animals for years!
Pigs are among the smartest animals on Earth! They can learn tricks as quickly as dogs!
A pig's snout never stops working! It's their primary tool for exploring and understanding the world!
Pigs can run 11 miles per hour! They can also swim surprisingly well despite their bulk!
Pigs don't sweat! They roll in mud to cool down because they can't perspire through their skin!
Ducklings can swim just 2 hours after hatching! They're born with a water-repellent coating on their feathers!
A duck's quack doesn't echo! This is a scientific fact that's still not fully understood!
Ducks can sleep with one eye open! Half their brain stays alert while the other half rests!
Some ducks can fly at speeds over 60 miles per hour! They migrate thousands of miles each year!
A turtle's shell is part of its skeleton! It's made of over 50 bones including ribs and spine!
Some sea turtles can hold their breath for up to 7 hours while sleeping underwater!
The sex of some turtle babies is determined by temperature! Cooler nests produce more males!
Turtles have existed for over 200 million years! They outlived dinosaurs and many other ancient creatures!
Polar bear fur isn't actually white! Each hair shaft is transparent and reflects light to appear white!
A polar bear's skin is black! This helps them absorb and retain heat from the sun!
Polar bears can swim for days without stopping! They've been known to swim over 200 miles!
Their fur is so insulated that polar bears can overheat in summer! They often rest in shade or snow!
Lobsters have blue blood! Their blood contains copper-based hemocyanin instead of iron-based hemoglobin!
A lobster's teeth are in its stomach! Three teeth-like structures grind food before it reaches the intestines!
Lobsters can live for over 100 years! They only become weaker when they molt their shells!
Lobsters can regenerate lost limbs! They can regrow legs, claws, and even eyes over time!
Only male peacocks display their colorful feathers! Females choose mates based on feather quality and size!
A peacock's train feathers can be 6 feet long! They shed these feathers every year after mating season!
Peacocks can fly, but only for short distances! They usually fly to escape predators or roost in trees!
A peacock's call is surprisingly harsh! It sounds more like a scream than a beautiful song!
Squirrels plant thousands of trees every year! They forget where they buried about 30% of their acorns!
A squirrel's front teeth never stop growing! They must chew constantly to keep them worn down!
Squirrels can rotate their ankles 180 degrees! This helps them climb down trees headfirst!
Baby squirrels are called kittens! A mother squirrel can have up to 8 babies in one litter!
A bee visits 50 to 100 flowers during one collection trip! Bees can travel up to 5 miles from their hive!
Honeybees can recognize human faces! They can remember them for at least two days!
A single bee produces about 1/12th of a teaspoon of honey in its lifetime! It takes 2 million flowers!
Bees communicate through dance! The waggle dance tells other bees the direction and distance of flowers!
Goldfish can remember things for at least 3 months! Their bad memory reputation is completely unfair!
A goldfish's attention span is about 9 seconds! But scientists say they can be trained to tell time!
Goldfish can see more colors than humans! They have four types of cone cells in their eyes!
A goldfish can live over 40 years! The oldest known goldfish lived to 43 years old!
A camel can drink up to 40 gallons of water at once! Their bodies are perfectly designed for desert life!
Camels have three eyelids and two rows of eyelashes! This protects their eyes from sand and sun!
A camel's hump stores fat, not water! They can survive for weeks without food using this reserve!
Camels can close their nostrils completely! This prevents sand from entering their nose during sandstorms!
Chameleons can move their eyes independently! Each eye can rotate 180 degrees separately!
A chameleon changes color using special cells called chromatophores! They change color to communicate and regulate temperature!
Chameleons have tongues that can be twice the length of their body! They can catch insects in 1/16th of a second!
Chameleons don't have ears, but they're not deaf! They can detect vibrations through their jawbone!
The narwhal's spiral tusk is actually a tooth! It can grow up to 10 feet long!
Narwhals can dive over a mile deep! They hold their breath for up to 25 minutes!
Narwhals can make over 30 different sounds! They use clicks, whistles, and knocks to communicate!
The Inuit people call narwhals the unicorns of the sea! Their tusk was once sold as unicorn horns!
The platypus is one of the few mammals that lay eggs instead of giving birth to live young!
Platypus males have venomous spurs on their hind legs! The venom can cause severe pain in humans!
A platypus hunts by sensing electric fields! It can detect the muscle contractions of hidden prey!
When scientists first saw a platypus specimen, they thought it was a hoax! It was sewn together from different animals!
A wolf's howl can be heard up to 10 miles away! They howl to communicate with pack members!
Wolves can run up to 40 miles per hour! They can maintain this speed for miles!
Each wolf has a unique howl! Scientists can identify individual wolves by their howl alone!
Wolves mate for life! A bonded pair will only breed with each other until one dies!
Bats are the only mammals that can truly fly! Their wingspan can reach up to 6 feet in some species!
Little brown bats can eat up to 1,000 mosquitoes in an hour! They're essential for pest control!
Bats navigate in complete darkness using echolocation! They emit sounds that bounce back as echoes!
A bat can live for over 30 years! Some species can survive temperatures below freezing while hibernating!
Rhino horns are made of keratin, the same protein as human fingernails and hair! They never stop growing!
Despite their massive size, rhinos can run up to 40 miles per hour! They're surprisingly fast!
Rhinos have poor eyesight but an excellent sense of smell! They can detect predators from miles away!
A group of rhinos is called a crash! They often graze alongside birds that eat parasites on their skin!
Every zebra has a unique stripe pattern! Like human fingerprints, no two zebras are exactly alike!
Zebras sleep standing up! They only lie down when they're in a safe area with other zebras watching!
A zebra's mane is entirely black! Unlike horses, zebras have erect manes that stand straight up!
Zebras can run up to 40 miles per hour! They use a bouncing gait called a trot when not fleeing predators!
Leopards can drag prey three times their own body weight into trees! They store food away from scavengers!
A leopard's spots are called rosettes because they look like roses! Each leopard has a unique pattern!
Leopards are incredibly adaptable! They live in forests, mountains, grasslands, and even deserts!
Leopards can leap 20 feet forward in a single bound! They're one of the most athletic big cats!
Snowy owls have feathers covering even their toes! This helps them survive in Arctic temperatures of -50°C!
Unlike most owls, snowy owls are primarily diurnal! They hunt during the day because the sun never sets in summer!
Snowy owls can swallow small prey whole! They cough up pellets containing bones and fur!
Male snowy owls get whiter with age! Females always have more dark feathers, even when fully mature!
An eagle's eyesight is 8 times more powerful than a human's! They can spot prey from miles away!
Eagles can see in color and can detect ultraviolet light! This helps them track prey and find fish!
A bald eagle's nest can weigh up to 2 tons! They add new materials each year and reuse the same nest for decades!
Eagles fly at altitudes up to 15,000 feet! They can spot rabbits from 2 miles away!
Alligators can go through 3,000 teeth in their lifetime! They replace lost teeth continuously!
An alligator's bite force is over 2,000 pounds! That's one of the strongest bites in the animal kingdom!
Alligators can hold their breath for over an hour! They slow their heart rate to conserve oxygen!
Baby alligators make a chirping sound before hatching! Mother alligators gently crack the eggs in their mouths!
Hippos can run 20 miles per hour despite their massive size! They're considered one of Africa's most dangerous animals!
Hippos secrete a natural sunscreen! Their red-colored mucus protects their skin from the harsh African sun!
Despite living mostly in water, hippos can't actually swim! They push themselves off the bottom!
A hippo's mouth can open up to 4 feet wide! Their teeth can be up to 20 inches long!
Crocodiles can hold their breath for over an hour! They slow their heartbeat to 2-3 beats per minute underwater!
A crocodile's bite force is over 3,700 pounds! Even stronger than the T-Rex dinosaur!
Crocodiles can climb trees! Young crocodiles especially climb to bask in the sun for warmth!
Crocodiles have the most sophisticated heart in the animal kingdom! They can redirect blood flow to their lungs when underwater!
